Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems begins with spearphishing to gain initial access, followed by lateral movement and manipulation of critical infrastructure to disrupt operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure and potential environmental harm

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The threat actor gathers public information about the target organization, including WHOIS records and contact details, to identify potential vulnerabilities and personnel for targeted attacks.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attacker compromises servers within the same industry to establish a foothold, enabling future attacks against similar targets.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: A tailored phishing email containing a malicious attachment is sent to employees, exploiting social engineering to install malware and gain initial network access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker exploits unpatched SCADA or PLC interfaces exposed to the internet to execute arbitrary code and establish persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Malware schedules recurring tasks to maintain access and re-establish connections if detected, ensuring continued presence in the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker leverages default credentials or misconfigured services on industrial controllers to escalate privileges and gain deeper access.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Logs and artifacts are deleted from infected systems to evade detection by basic SIEM tools, masking the attacker's activities.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Passwords are extracted from memory to enable lateral movement across the network, allowing access to additional systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps shared drives and directories to locate sensitive operational technology (OT) assets and plan further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, the attacker accesses other systems within the OT network via RDP or SSH to expand their control.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Configuration files, control logic, and sensor data from SCADA systems are harvested for analysis and potential exploitation.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Stolen data is encrypted and transmitted via covert channels embedded in legitimate traffic to external command-and-control servers.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Chemical treatment parameters in water systems are altered to cause contamination or service disruptions, directly impacting public safety.

Scenario.summary: Operation Sharpshooter begins with spearphishing to breach water/wastewater networks, escalates privileges, and manipulates critical infrastructure to cause environmental harm.
